lesson: A freezing-cold refrigerant line with mix that stays warm means something is insulating the cylinder — on a granita machine that’s sugar scale. The temperature split is the tell; regassing changes nothing.
symptoms: Slushie is taking hours to freeze down.
Compressor is running hot and long.
Bowls look full and augers are turning.

tests.temp-probe: The refrigerant line reads −9 °C, but the mix is stuck at 4 °C and barely moving down.
tests.inspect-beater: A white sugar crust is building on the wetted metal — bowl skins and the cylinder flanks wear a matte film.

flavour: Scrubbed the sugar crust. The cold can finally get through.
authenticityNote: If the operators do not scrub the cylinder surface during sanitisation, a microscopic layer of sugar and milk solids (in dairy slushies) or hard water minerals will build up. This scale acts as a thermal insulator, preventing heat exchange between the refrigerant and the slush.
